name: Saguenay
summary: Saguenay is a French-speaking Canadian town tucked beside a giant fjord carved by Ice Age glaciers. White beluga whales swim in the cold water year-round, and a tall white statue watches over the cliffs. Locals call themselves Bleuets because the wild blueberries here grow huge.
funFacts: The Saguenay Fjord stretches about 105 kilometres long and is one of the rare fjords ships can sail deep into right in North America.
Beluga whales are completely white and chatty, and Saguenay is the only place in Quebec where they live all year, even under winter ice.
A 7.5-metre wooden statue called Notre-Dame du Saguenay was hauled up Cap Trinité cliff in 1881 after a merchant survived falling through river ice.
Locals proudly call themselves Bleuets, meaning blueberries, because wild blueberries grow so plump here that one regional pie uses over 120 pounds of fruit.
The fjord was scooped out by huge glaciers during the last Ice Age, ending around 10,000 years ago, leaving cliffs that drop straight into salty seawater.
thingsToDo: Cruise to Cap Trinité on a fjord boat to spot the white Notre-Dame statue perched 180 metres above the water on a rocky terrace.
Watch the famous cruise welcome ceremony at La Baie pier, where costumed locals, music, and giant projections greet your ship as it docks.
Visit La Pulperie de Chicoutimi, a riverside museum built inside a real 1896 pulp mill with twelve interactive stops about regional history.
Drive to Tadoussac and take a small zodiac boat into the marine park to look for beluga whales, minkes, and sometimes blue whales.
Explore the Fjord Museum in La Baie, where a giant aquarium and touch pool let you meet sturgeon and other fish from the Saguenay.
Taste fresh blueberry pie or chocolate-covered Bleuets in a Saguenay shop, and try traditional poutine and tourtière from a local bakery.

localWord.meaning: Blueberry, and also the proud nickname locals from Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean call themselves

images.alt: A sweeping view over the deep blue waters and forested cliffs of the Saguenay Fjord.
The white Notre-Dame du Saguenay statue perched high on the towering Cap Trinité cliff above the fjord.
A friendly white beluga whale swims beside her little calf in the St. Lawrence near the Saguenay.
A pretty panorama of Tadoussac village with its red-roofed buildings beside the sparkling bay.
A wide view across the city of Saguenay where the river winds past colourful neighbourhoods and bridges.
